content.claim: Rural areas in Hungary's North-Eastern, South-Western, and Western regions have uneven public transport access; frequency and scheduling in small settlements make public transport impractical for daily school commutes, with a significant gap between Budapest and rural areas in barrier-free transport infrastructure development.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Specific route density and frequency data not quantified in research notes
Gap between Budapest and rural areas is asserted but not measured against concrete service standards
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
